methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DroneForge is a pre-seed stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $2.5M to date. The comparable universe consists of five private frontier-tech peers in drones and autonomous systems—including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air—though no public comps exist in this nascent sector.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($2.5M) as a proxy for current implied valuation, the standard pre-seed to next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $6.25M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Drones & Autonomous sector—five total peers exist, but none met the filter criteria of at least three datapoints at equivalent stage or valuation tier.

The triangulated range supported by this single-method analysis spans $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M. This range reflects reasonable bounds around the stage-progression result but should be interpreted with caution: the single methodological input and sparse peer set create material uncertainty in the valuation. Any refined estimate would benefit from either secured financial performance data (revenue, burn rate) enabling forward-looking methods, or expanded comparable data from adjacent frontier-tech hardware or autonomous systems stages.
